# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Python image as a parent image
FROM python:3.8-slim

# Set the working directory in the container to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the requirements file into the working directory
COPY requirements.txt .

# Install the dependencies
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy the rest of the code (the app) into the working directory
COPY . .

# Expose ports 80, 8080, 5432 and 6379 to make them accessible from outside the container
EXPOSE 80
EXPOSE 8080
EXPOSE 5432
EXPOSE 6379

# Run the command to start the services when the container launches
CMD ["gunicorn", "app:app", "--workers=4", "--bind=0.0.0.0:80"]
CMD ["python", "-m", "flask_api", "--host=0.0.0.0", "--port=8080"]
CMD ["psql", "-d", "mydb", "-U", "myuser", "-p", "5432"]
CMD ["redis-server"]
